revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:廣幡八幡宮 (柏市)]]
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Hirohata Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:廣幡八幡宮神社 拝殿.JPG | location = 895 Masuo, {{ill|Kashiwa|en|Kashiwa|qid=Q319743|lt=Kashiwa|1=Kashiwa}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|qid=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.838881|N|139.989272|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Foundation = Traditionally during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Uda|en|Emperor Uda|qid=Q349267|lt=Emperor Uda|1=Emperor Uda}} (887–897 CE) | Shrine Rank = Village shrine (村社) | deity = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|qid=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} | Main hall style = Gongen-zukuri | location = {{ill|Kashiwa|qid=Q319743|1=Kashiwa}} }} '''Hirohata Hachiman Shrine''' (廣幡八幡宮, Hirohata Hachiman-gū) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kashiwa|en|Kashiwa|qid=Q319743|lt=Kashiwa|1=Kashiwa}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|qid=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}. Under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qid=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a village shrine (村社). [[File:HirohataHatiman.jpg|thumb|Approach path]]
== Origin == According to the shrine legend, it was established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Uda|en|Emperor Uda|qid=Q349267|lt=Emperor Uda|1=Emperor Uda}} (887–897 CE) as the "First Guardian Shrine of Shimōsa Province" and an imperial shrine designated by Emperor Uda.
== History == * Between 887 and 897 CE: consecration_year, later fell into disuse. * {{ill|1193|en|1193|qid=Q19741|lt=1193|1=1193}} (Kenkyū 4): Reconstructed. * {{ill|1649|en|1649|qid=Q6945|lt=1649|1=1649}} (Keian 3): Received a ten-koku red-seal land grant from {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|en|Tokugawa Iemitsu|qid=Q314481|lt=Tokugawa Iemitsu|1=Tokugawa Iemitsu}}. * {{ill|1758|en|1758|qid=Q7639|lt=1758|1=1758}} (Hōreki 7): A stone [[Torii|gate]] was donated by Fujiwara no Masatomi, Governor of {{ill|Hōki Province|en|Hōki Province|qid=Q1053703|lt=Hōki Province|1=Hōki Province}}. * {{ill|1993|en|1993|qid=Q2065|lt=1993|1=1993}} (Heisei 5): Main hall rebuilt to commemorate the 800th anniversary of the shrine's reconstruction.
== Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|qid=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|qid=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashihime no Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}} * [[Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayorihime no Mikoto]] * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|qid=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai|1=Emperor Chūai}} * [[Takenouchi no Sukune]]
== Access == * 4 km (approx. 50 minutes on foot) from {{ill|Kashiwa Station|en|Kashiwa Station|qid=Q865156|lt=Kashiwa Station|1=Kashiwa Station}} on the {{ill|Jōban Line|en|Jōban Line|qid=Q1369263|lt=Jōban Line|1=Jōban Line}} ({{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|qid=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company|1=East Japan Railway Company}}). * 1.5 km (approx. 19 minutes on foot) from {{ill|Masuo Station|en|Masuo Station|qid=Q6785709|lt=Masuo Station|1=Masuo Station}} on the [[:en:Tobu Noda Line|Tobu Noda Line]].
== See Also == * [[:en:Kashiwa, Chiba|Kashiwa, Chiba]] * [[Hachiman]]
== External Links == * [http://www.hirohatahachimangu.com/index.html Official website of Hirohata Hachiman Shrine]
{{shinto-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|廣幡八幡宮 (柏市)|version=69335423|comment=Imported full ja history}}
